0x00.name: Not
0x00.description: Or in other words, a TRUE becomes a FALSE, and a FALSE becomes a TRUE. Note: If placed inside an If block, may yank passing command runs into the If block at that point in time even when they should be skipping over it. Tested by putting a SoundEffect/Pause combo immediately before and after the Not...

0x01.name: Unknown01
0x01.description: World Map Script 0x01 If Script Data is equal to loaded integer, continue script 80091238 - World Map Scripts

0x02.name: Unknown02
0x02.description: Once encountered, it jumps to returns false, then it checks the given Variable's value for > 0 and proceeds in the same fashion. Variable ID containing the value to perform an If comparison on.

0x03.name: Else
0x03.description: Once encountered, it jumps to If it parses it as a result of previous instructions returning false, then it executes the instructions below itself.

0x04.name: EndIf
0x04.description: World Map Script 0x04 If Sprite Set Unit is present on the team, continue This page was last edited on 29 June 2020, at 13:21.

0x05.name: Switch
0x05.description: Executes a Switch statement based off the value in the corresponding variable. Variable ID containing the value to be verified by the

0x06.name: Case
0x06.description: If the assigned Value matches the value of the variable specified in the previous instruction, then it executes the code below until it encounters a Otherwise, it will attempt the

0x07.name: Unknown07
0x07.description: Executes the instructions below itself if previous

0x08.name: Unknown08
0x08.description: Once encountered, immediately jumps to the next

0x09.name: Unknown09
0x09.description: Does absolutely nothing. Acts as a jumping target for

0x0A.name: DefineSub
0x0A.description: Does absolutely nothing on its own. Acts as a destination for ID with which to call the sub using

0x0B.name: Unknown0B
0x0B.description: Returns to the event position following the last

0x0C.name: Unknown0C
0x0C.description: Scans the entire event from the beginning to find a with a matching ID, then executes the code from there. Stores the event position of the event instruction that follows itself (CallSub) as a return address, which will be used once the event will encounter

0x0D.name: GoTo
0x0D.description: Scans the entire event from the beginning to find a GoToTarget with an identical Target parameter, and executes the code from there. Identifier that must match the value specified in

0x0E.name: Unknown0E
0x0E.description: World Map Script 0x0E This page was last edited on 3 March 2025, at 20:50.

0x0F.name: ScriptEnd
0x0F.description: World Map Script 0x0F This page was last edited on 3 March 2025, at 20:52.

0x10.name: DisplayMessage

params.name: message_id
params.description: Text message ID from scenario PZD

0x10.description: Display a dialogue message with speaker portrait and balloon

0x11.name: AnimationRequest
0x11.description: This instruction is used load a humanoid shaped sprite off EVTCHR.BIN on a sprite using the original unit's palette. Unit ID: ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. Load an animation sequence from the corresponding SEQ file for a unit, or an EVTCHR Animation. See lower for EVTCHR Animations.

0x12.name: WaitAnimationEnd
0x12.description: World Map Script 0x12 This page was last edited on 3 March 2025, at 22:51.

0x13.name: JumpMap
0x13.description: Changes the map the event is currently played in. Not recommended for use. If you wish to change the map in a way that you have considerably more control, see At Main Gate of Igros Castle

0x14.name: PlayTrack
0x14.description: World Map Script 0x14 and 0x15 This page was last edited on 3 March 2025, at 20:36.

0x15.name: Unknown15
0x15.description: Wipes the damage, status and other temporary calculations on all units on the field, allowing Effects to never display any damage/healing numbers when called with Note: Currently bugged. Resets a ton of other stats as well, including non-temporary ones. It's more reliable to zero out the Battle Stat...

0x16.name: WaitTask
0x16.description: Pause Event Instruction and other surrounding currently-unknown functions. void BATTLE_pause_event_instruction(void)

0x17.name: Unknown17
0x17.description: World Map Script 0x17 This page was last edited on 14 December 2012, at 00:52.

0x18.name: ChangeEffect
0x18.description: Uses an ability or effect on target Unit.  Only one Effect can play at a time or the game will freeze. Preview videos for each Effect can be found here: http://ffhacktics.com/smf/index.php?topic=4830.0

0x19.name: MoveCamera
0x19.description: Move camera to a position with rotation, tilt, and zoom

0x1A.name: MoveAmbient
0x1A.description: Modifies the color of the map's lighting. You must use at least one one of the following; x00 will not work: After the first glow, it resets previously set blends and turns negative colors into positive colors (ex: -80,0,0 becomes +80,0,0)

0x1C.name: ChangeFrameRate
0x1C.description: This instruction determines the speed the event will play. It is only used alongside Lucavi transformation and death Effects to slow them down. Some effects (such as Quake) run too quickly, even after using this command. There doesn't seem to be a way to make them match the same speed they run at wh...

0x1D.name: _CameraFusionStart
0x1D.description: Place many camera instructions in this block make them transition smoothly.

0x1E.name: _CameraFusionEnd
0x1E.description: Place many camera instructions in this block make them transition smoothly.

0x1F.name: _Focus
0x1F.description: The next Camera instruction will automatically center between Unit 1 and Unit 2. If the two are the same unit, it focuses on that single unit. placed after this instruction (or after You can set the Travel Speed of the Camera instruction using

0x20.name: Unknown20
0x20.description: World Map Script 0x20 This page was last edited on 3 March 2025, at 21:34.

0x21.name: PlaySound
0x21.description: Terminate currently playing sound created by this instruction & plays the indexed sound once. Remove Item or Ability Anti-Clockwise Rotation*

0x22.name: PlayMusic
0x22.description: Toggle between the first and second song assigned in ATTACK.OUT for the scenario. Volume based on original level of the song. +000 : 0% volume

0x23.name: Unknown23
0x23.description: World Map Script 0x23 This page was last edited on 3 March 2025, at 21:35.

0x24.name: Unknown24
0x24.description: World Map Script 0x24 This page was last edited on 3 March 2025, at 21:35.

0x25.name: Unknown25
0x25.description: World Map Script 0x25 This page was last edited on 3 March 2025, at 21:35.
0x25.routine_header: unknown, saves some data to 0x80166072 and 0x80166074

0x26.name: Unknown26
0x26.description: World Map Script 0x26 This page was last edited on 3 March 2025, at 21:36.

0x27.name: RewriteMap
0x27.description: Reloads the map with new, currently stored settings such as map arrangement (Variable x0030), weather and daytime. Map arrangements and daytime can be viewed using map2gl. Notable uses of map arrangements: Removing/adding chairs/benches/tables

0x28.name: MoveToPanel
0x28.description: Makes a unit attempt to walk to a given tile. If the unit cannot reach destination, it will only enter walking state and not move at all. Units in events have 3 Jump regardless of equipped or innate abilities using this instruction, but

0x29.name: WaitCharacterMove
0x29.description: Resumes instructions when the given unit arrives at destination. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x2A.name: _BlockStart
0x2A.description: A block is a portion of the event played in a separate process. When the game finds a block, it will start executing it and will also resume the event whatever there is after the block at the same time. Only a few select instructions work in Blocks:

0x2B.name: _BlockEnd
0x2B.description: A block is a portion of the event played in a separate process. When the game finds a block, it will start executing it and will also resume the event whatever there is after the block at the same time. Only a few select instructions work in Blocks:

0x2C.name: Direction2_0
0x2C.description: A copy of {53} FaceUnit with one a certain value set to 0 instead of 1. Visually, there doesn't seem to be any difference with the other. Makes unit(s) rotate to face a specified unit. The rotation direction is the shortest one (Clockwise as default). ID of the unit specified in the ENTD that will b...

0x2D.name: Direction
0x2D.description: The unit will rotate to a given direction. Unit ID: ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. Check the Map section to know what direction to use.

0x2E.name: FadeGradation
0x2E.description: Creates a gradient background gradually by ignoring its current state using RGB. Battle and Effect safe. Red value of the RGB color. Green value of the RGB color.

0x2F.name: Unknown2F
0x2F.description: This instruction allows looping a block until the defined variable reaches, or is set to 0. It has to be placed inside a block in order to work. A block left running will prevent the event from ending! 000: Loops indefinitely (the variable has to be manually set to 0 to end the loop)

0x31.name: ChangeGradation
0x31.description: Colors Background to new value based on the current coloration, allowing better blending but ineffective on pure-black backgrounds. You can mix the RGB color with any of the preset colors: Red value of the RGB color.

0x32.name: SetCharacterColor
0x32.description: Colors a unit gradually depending on its current state. (+127,-128,-128) is red and (+127,+127,+127) is white while (+000,+000,+000) returns the unit to its original color. Unit ID: ID of the unit specified in the ENTD.

0x33.name: ChangeMapClut
0x33.description: Colors entire map specified colors. (-128,-128,-128) is black and (+127,+127,+127) is white while (+000,+000,+000) returns the map to its original color. Instead of setting RGB values, you can pick any of the preset colors:

0x37.name: Unknown37
0x37.description: Final Fantasy Hacktics Wiki Event Instruction 0x37 This page was last edited on 10 May 2025, at 14:34.

0x38.name: _FocusSpeed
0x38.description: Focus Speed Event Instruction #   ROUTINE: FOCUS SPEED EVENT INSTRUCTION #           r4 = (params) Event instruction parameters

0x39.name: WaitCharacterMoveWotL
0x39.description: Final Fantasy Hacktics Wiki Event Instruction 0x39 This page was last edited on 10 May 2025, at 14:31.

0x3A.name: WaitFileRead
0x3A.routine_header: unknown, runs Check File Still Loading routine and waits for it to finish
0x3A.description: Wait until a file finishes loading

0x3B.name: MoveSprite
0x3B.description: Smoothly move a sprite to a position over time

0x3C.name: ChangeWeather
0x3C.description: Change the weather effect (rain, snow, etc.)

0x3D.name: DisappearUnit
0x3D.description: Removes a unit from the field and memory entirely. This differs from BlueRemoveUnit in that it works immediately, allowing you to Add another unit/sprite right away, and that a Removed unit doesn't award its War Trophy. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD.

0x3E.name: SetChangePaletteData
0x3E.description: +TIMER Colors the whole screen, in different possible ways. The Initial Color is applied immediately, while the Target Color gradually changes with a given amount of time assigned. To achieve black or any darker color, you need to set the

0x40.name: ChangeMapSTP
0x40.routine_header: unknown, something to do with tile data

0x41.name: StartShake
0x41.description: Cause a seism with given parameters, but it does not control any sound related to it. Strength of the seism. Any higher value would look too weird as there are only two main frames.

0x43.name: _CallFunction
0x43.description: Can call various different functions to alter many different things in the game. https://ffhacktics.com/smf/index.php?topic=10124.msg193711#msg193711 )  NOTE: Skipped if Var 0x1FC (Brave Story Event?) is flagged. NOTE 2: Behaves differently if this is Event ID 0x12C (vanilla's final event)

0x44.name: _Draw
0x44.description: Draws a loaded unit that is currently not being displayed. In vanilla, if this command is used during the event ID reserved for the Zalera pre-battle event, the unit it's used on will immediately have its status animations applied to it. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD.

0x45.name: LoadAnimation
0x45.description: Adds a unit to the event that is not currently loaded. This instruction can be either placed inside a block, or have it followed by

0x46.name: DeactivateAnimation
0x46.description: Erase a unit whose sprite is currently being displayed. Unit can be re-drawn later. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x47.name: ActivateAnimation
0x47.description: Adds a fake unit on the map which can be mostly controlled like a regular unit, with some exceptions. In battle, the unit cannot take action or be targeted, and units will be able to walk through it. You can use any spritesheet you want, but be warned: Adding a new spritesheet will leave you with th...

0x48.name: WaitLoadAnimation
0x48.description: Waits until a normal or ghost unit is loaded into the event before resuming.

0x49.name: ActivateAnimationStart
0x49.description: Creates an independently running block inside the event to load one or many units in the game. Does not slow down the event in any way, but make sure to use {4B} WaitAddUnitEnd before using instructions on the new units. There is no need to use {48} WaitAddUnit after each unit inside this block.

0x4A.name: ActivateAnimationEnd
0x4A.description: Creates an independently running block inside the event to load one or many units in the game. Does not slow down the event in any way, but make sure to use {4B} WaitAddUnitEnd before using instructions on the new units. There is no need to use {48} WaitAddUnit after each unit inside this block.

0x4B.name: WaitActivateAnimation
0x4B.description: Waits until a currently running {49} AddUnitStart/{4A} AddUnitEnd block has completed before resuming.

0x4C.name: JumpMap2
0x4C.description: Fades to black, then changes the map, reloading all the settings such as Weather or State. Just like as with a fresh event, reveal the map using This instruction will mess up with the dialogue when recruiting units, and it can prevent dialog boxes from properly displaying if

0x4D.name: _Reveal
0x4D.description: Progressively reveals the screen to unveil the scene. Required to start a new scenario. WARNING: Ending an event before a Reveal command has finished will automatically kick the player out to the World Map, even if another event is supposed to play. Also, doing a second Reveal before one Reveal ends...

0x4E.name: SetAnimationShadow
0x4E.description: Remove or add back the shadow of a unit. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x4F.name: SetDaytime
0x4F.description: Stores day or night for the next time you will load/reload a map.

0x50.name: SetFace
0x50.description: Sets the column that will be used to load a custom portrait from EVTFACE.BIN for the following Dialog Boxes. Use this instruction before with the given Portrait Column and type the Portrait Row in the

0x51.name: _ChangeDialog
0x51.description: Changes the content of a Dialog Box or closes it. Only seems to work with Dialog Types x8X through xEX. For more information, see the Dialog Type section of

0x53.name: Direction2_1
0x53.description: Makes unit(s) rotate to face a specified unit. The rotation direction is the shortest one (Clockwise as default). ID of the unit specified in the ENTD that will be faced from the This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x54.name: StartModelAnimation
0x54.description: Use an object that has a 3D animation. 002 Back Gate of Lesalia 036 Church Outside of Town

0x55.name: StartVRAMAnimation
0x55.description: Changes the state of an object on the map. Most maps do not have objects that have many states, and there is no easy way to know which are. In some cases (perhaps all cases, as this is untested thoroughly), the ID references a Textured Animation ID as defined in a

0x56.name: WaitModelAnimation
0x56.description: Waits until all activated 3D objects are done animating before resuming.

0x57.name: WaitVRAMAnimation
0x57.description: Waits for an object being used to complete its cycle before resuming.

0x58.name: LoadEventCharacter
0x58.description: Loads an EVTCHR slot from the CD to temporary memory. This instruction takes time, and it's recommended to use afterwards. You'll then also need to use

0x59.name: ActivateEventCharacter
0x59.description: Saves the loaded EVTCHR Slot to a specific block in order to use its frames and animations. Must use the same Memory Block as the previous Note: If both blocks are in use, normal unit animations past x003B may no longer work.

0x5A.name: DeactivateEventCharacter
0x5A.description: Allows to save an EVTCHR slot once more in the given memory block. To replace an EVTCHR, this must be used before If you are just trying to Clear out an EVTCHR for any reason, such as issues with UnitAnim in a event, on attack frames. You can simply put

0x5B.name: DisposeEventCharacter
0x5B.description: Allows to load an EVTCHR slot once more in the given memory block. To replace an EVTCHR, this must be used before If you are just trying to Clear out an EVTCHR for any reason, such as issues with UnitAnim in a event, on attack frames. You can simply put

0x5D.name: DeactivateCompressedAnimation
0x5D.description: Unknown — deactivates compressed animation, saves param to 0x80173F48

0x5E.name: DisposeMusic
0x5E.description: Terminates the currently playing song immediately. This instruction is never used, and it is hard to tell the function of this value, if it has any. It IS saved to 0x80165FCA, for what that's worth.

0x5F.name: SetAnimationPosition
0x5F.description: Warps a unit to a new location instantly. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x60.name: FadeMusic
0x60.description: Progressively fade sounds and music until they stop playing completely. Warning: This does NOT work on sounds generated by Never used, always x00.

0x62.name: Unknown62
0x62.routine_header: unknown, saves some values to the unit's X, Y, and elevation/facing
0x62.description: Unknown — modifies unit X, Y, and elevation/facing

0x63.name: SetMoveCameraFlags
0x63.description: Affects the speed curve of Camera and Focus instructions. We likely do not fully understand the full scope of the effects of its parameters yet. Previous notes by Nyzer

0x64.name: WaitDirection
0x64.description: Waits for a unit to finish rotating/facing another unit before resuming. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x65.name: WaitDirectionAll
0x65.description: Waits for every unit to finish rotating/facing another unit before resuming.

0x66.name: SetPresentClutDataAsDefault
0x66.description: Event Instruction 0x66 These values are consistent when this routine is called by both Event Instruction Main Routine and 000f6f18 - 000f929c. # if r6 =/= 1 (never runs?)

0x67.name: DeadCode67
0x67.notes: All 10 uses are after EventEnd. Gibbed: Invalid (no handler). Vestigial/unreachable.
0x67.description: OPEN.BIN Button Input Related 1 (0x67d84) #       // Perform reset sequence? This page was last edited on 23 May 2025, at 22:24.

0x68.name: SetAnimationHorizontalFlip
0x68.description: Mirrors a sprite and all its animations/ETVCHR as if they were facing left instead of right, and vice versa. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x69.name: Direction4
0x69.description: Makes unit(s) rotate to face a specified tile. The rotation direction is the shortest one (Clockwise as default). Unit ID: ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. X Coordinate of the target tile.

0x6A.name: FadeSoundEffect
0x6A.description: #   ROUTINE: ? 0x6aed0 #           r4 = (p1) Struct data #               First 8 entries are 4-byte values, and final 3 are 1-byte values.

0x6B.name: PlaySoundEffect
0x6B.description: Final Fantasy Hacktics Wiki Toggle {6B} BGSound subsection Plays the indexed background sound effect.

0x6C.name: SetAnimationColorChangeOff
0x6C.description: Final Fantasy Hacktics Wiki Event Instruction 0x6C and 0x6D This page was last edited on 10 May 2025, at 14:33.

0x6D.name: SetAnimationColorChangeOn
0x6D.description: Unknown — paired with 0x6C, related to animation color

0x6F.name: WaitMoveSprite
0x6F.description: Wait for a unit to complete its custom movement before resuming. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x70.name: JumpToPanel
0x70.description: The given unit will jump up or down 1-4 tiles distance. It works perfectly for jumping down, but might look weird when trying to jump to tiles too height. Does not have an elevation parameter. If the height difference is very minimal, the unit will just walk instead. If the target tile is off the ma...

0x71.name: RaiseAnimationPriority
0x71.description: Unknown — related to Misc ID processing

0x72.name: ForceStop
0x72.description: Final Fantasy Hacktics Wiki Event Instruction 0x72 This page was last edited on 24 November 2025, at 10:12.

0x73.name: MoveCamera2
0x73.description: Seems to control parameters in the cameras. Used in the random battle camera to pan out at the end.
0x73.routine_header: unknown, uses a jal shared by CameraFusion

0x74.name: Unknown74
0x74.description: No operation (but with extra initialization steps)

0x76.name: StartWipe
0x76.description: Create a dark screen that allows units to join your party, display winning conditions and save your party back to formation. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function. Controls the starting point where the expansion of the dark screen starts and also i...

0x77.name: StopWipe
0x77.description: Removes the dark screen with the same parameters it was called with.

0x78.name: _DisplayConditions
0x78.description: Display a Winning condition, Congratulations, War Trophies or Bonus Money received after the battle. Also saves JP/XP/etc. from deployed units to the roster * Condition 0x02 shows "THIS GAME IS COMPLETE!" if Var x27 matches the event ID of the Altima battle before the DarkScreen command is run (0x14...

0x79.name: _WalkToAnim
0x79.description: Applies a normal or an EVTCHR animation to a given unit when it arrives at destination. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. The EVTCHR sprite will be pasted directly over the unit. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x7A.name: EraseUnit
0x7A.description: Removes the first unit which matches the given Special Job ID from the player's roster. Cannot remove the unit in the first roster slot (i.e. Ramza); it starts checking from Slot 2 instead of slot 1. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x7B.name: TutorialButtonWait
0x7B.routine_header: (TutorialButtonWait) Waits for the specified button press (Tutorials/Records only)
0x7B.description: Waits for the specified button to be pressed before continuing. Only intended for Tutorial or Brave Story "Records" events due to the button press value requiring Variable 0x01FC to be flagged in order to be saved to the checked location. While the game will register L3/R3 and even the analog stick ...

0x7C.name: StopAllEffects
0x7C.description: Final Fantasy Hacktics Wiki Terminates the currently playing SoundEffect and BGSound immediately. See also: {21} SoundEffect ― {6A} EditBGSound ― {6B} BGSound

0x7D.name: DisplayChapter
0x7D.description: Gradually prints a graphic on the screen, and erases it the same way. Graphics displayed by this instruction must have faded away by the time the event ends! Use Use GraphicTimeout to set a timeout of make a graphic fade out immediately.

0x7E.name: WaitEventFlag
0x7E.description: Waits until a given value has reached at least a certain amount before resuming. Only ever used in the original game to resume after a certain amount of characters with Click here for the list of Variables

0x7F.name: SetEventCharacterClut
0x7F.description: Changes the palette of a character by loading one from an EVTCHR Block. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x80.name: RequestStandardAnimation
0x80.description: Make units start their walking animation, ready to fight. Even if not called, this instruction is automatically executed when a battle starts with 0 frames between units. Unit ID: ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. Time assigned in frames to complete the instruction. One frame is the equivalent o...

0x82.name: WaitLoadAnimation2
0x82.description: Unknown. Only ever used inside {49} AddUnitStart + {4A} AddUnitEnd blocks. Outside of those blocks, seems to simulate a brief Wait period. High tendency to break the game when {43} CallFunction(x06) is used afterwards. Even though the original game achieves this without crashing, the procedure is un...

0x83.name: _ChangeStats
0x83.description: ChangeStats Event Instruction Parameters: r4 = Event parameters pointer Offsets: 0x00 Affected Units

0x84.name: PlayJingle
0x84.description: Plays a song file. Don't get excited, the list is very short and mostly useless. Any other value will freeze the game.

0x85.name: ChangeTreasureFindDay
0x85.description: Sets the unlocking date of a treasure/land to the current day. To Unlock the Treasures/Lands themselves, use 1D : Longibunne Spear

0x86.name: EquipWeapon
0x86.description: Gives unit a temporary weapon to swing. Does not affect equipped items. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x87.name: UseGun
0x87.description: Adds an arrow or bolt the next time a unit that has a bow (arrow) or a crossbow (bolt) shoots with a SEQ animation of x53, x54 or x55. ID of the target specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x8A.name: WaitEffectLoad
0x8A.description: Must be used in a block to activate the animation of a previously set Effect file.

0x8B.name: PlayEffect
0x8B.description: Get Facing Short from Misc Data (Redirected from 0x8bf1c) Get Current Facing Short from Misc Data

0x8C.name: SetAnimationFlipDirection
0x8C.description: Makes the unit immediately rotate to a certain direction and execute an animation. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x8D.name: Unknown8D
0x8D.description: Allows performing comparisons on item quantities, as well as adding/subtracting item quantities in various ways. This takes into account the number of equipped items to make sure the regular inventory never exceeds 99 items even if you use Parameter x10 instead of x20. For the Fur Shop, the assumed ...

0x8E.name: WaitDisplayChapter
0x8E.description: Waits until a graphic generated by {7D} ShowGraphic is done animating, fully showing on the screen, before resuming.

0x8F.name: AllGrayFilter
0x8F.description: Apply/remove a grayscale filter over the screen

0x90.name: WaitActivePanel
0x90.gibbed_note: 90 - waits for active panel x/y to match args
0x90.description: TutorialCoordinateConfirm( Wait for Circle to be pressed while the cursor is at the specified coordinates. Only intended for Tutorial or Brave Story "Records" events due to the button press value requiring Variable 0x01FC to be flagged in order to be saved to the checked location.

0x91.name: DisplayMapTitle
0x91.description: Displays a 256x20 4bit image (EVENT\MAPTITLE.BIN) of the current map's title on the screen. Only Map 1 to 120 have a graphic assigned to them. Trying to display the title of other maps will result in rubbish. By default the graphic is printed horizontally centered, and right above the middle line ve...

0x92.name: _InflictStatus
0x92.description: Inflict a status on a given unit. The changes are visible even outside of battle. Statuses can be stacked, but sometimes need to be applied in the correct order; for example, Chicken needs to be applied before Float. Warning: Any event with an InflictStatus command will instantly apply it at the sta...

0x93.name: WriteEventFlag
0x93.routine_header: - advances the date upon returning to the world map
0x93.description: Advance the in-game date upon returning to world map

0x94.name: TeleportOut
0x94.description: Unit will teleport out of the field. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x95.name: Unknown95
0x95.description: 00143bd8 - 001449f8: Event Instruction Main Routine 1 | 001449fc - 00145750: Event Instruction Main Routine 2

0x96.name: _AppendMapState
0x96.description: Append map changes to be loaded during a {43} CallFunction event with an effect.

0x97.name: SetAnimationBrightColor
0x97.description: Resets the palette color of a unit to its original state. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x98.name: TeleportIn
0x98.description: Unit will teleport onto the field. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0x99.name: _BlueRemoveUnit
0x99.description: Remove a non-Charmed enemy team unit from the map with a blue hueing. This differs from RemoveUnit in that it takes longer, so you can't Add another unit/sprite in immediately afterwards, and that a BlueRemoved unit will still award their War Trophies at the end. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD...

0x9A.name: ResetTextIncrement
0x9A.description: Resets the "push" increment variable used to append text variable values. for more information on text variables.

0x9B.name: Unknown9B
0x9B.description: Pushes a value to the next text variable, starting from 0x00. Increases Push increment by 1. for more information on text variables.

0x9C.name: Unknown9C
0x9C.description: Pushes a variable's value to the next text variable, starting from 0x00. Increases Push increment by 1. for more information on text variables.

0x9D.name: Unknown9D
0x9D.description: Pushes a value to the specified Text Variable. for more information on Text Variables. Note: this command may be bugged if the value of a byte goes too high. See the

0x9E.name: Unknown9E
0x9E.description: Pushes a Variable's value to the specified Text Variable. for more information on Text Variables. Click here for a list of the Text Variables

0x9F.name: ButtonPressGoTo
0x9F.description: If all specified buttons are being pressed at the same time when executing this instruction, the event jumps to the specified GoToTarget. While the game will register L3/R3 and even the analog stick coordinates in some emulators, the original console is unable to, and thus those buttons should be av...

0xA0.name: LessThanEquals
0xA0.description: Less Than or Equal | Variable 0x0000 = If ( Variable 0x0000 <= Variable 0x0001 ) Compares Variables x0000 & x0001. If x0000's value is less than or equal to x0001's, x0000 is set to a value of 0001.

0xA1.name: GreaterThanEquals
0xA1.description: Greater Than or Equal | Variable 0x0000 = If ( Variable 0x0000 >= Variable 0x0001 ) Compares Variables x0000 & x0001. If x0000's value is greater than or equal to x0001's, x0000 is set to a value of 0001.

0xA2.name: Equals
0xA2.description: Compares Variables x0000 & x0001. If x0000's value is equal to x0001's, x0000 is set to a value of 0001. If not, x0000 is set to a value of 0000.

0xA3.name: NotEquals
0xA3.description: Compares Variables x0000 & x0001. If x0000's value is not equal to x0001's, x0000 is set to a value of 0001. If they are equal, x0000 is set to a value of 0000.

0xA4.name: LessThan
0xA4.description: Compares Variables x0000 & x0001. If x0000's value is less than (and equal to) x0001's, x0000 is set to a value of 0001.

0xA5.name: GreaterThan
0xA5.description: Compares Variables x0000 & x0001. If x0000's value is greater than (and equal to) x0001's, x0000 is set to a value of 0001.

0xA8.name: SetSpriteRenderParam
0xA8.gibbed_operands: U16=entity_id, U32=render_param. Writes U32 to anim_struct[343] via TIC render pipeline.

0xA9.name: InitSpriteRender
0xA9.gibbed_operands: Gibbed types: U16, U32 — U16 unused?

0xAA.name: SuspendBattleEngine
0xAA.description: Suspend the battle engine during events (TIC exclusive)

0xAB.name: DisplayBattleMessage
0xAB.description: Display battle message UI (TIC exclusive, enhanced mode)

0xAC.name: LUI
0xAC.description: Load Upper Immediate | Variable = Immediate Value << 16 Click here for the list of Variables Value that will be use along with the operand to change the address' value.

0xAE.name: ChangePostEffectLUT
0xAE.description: Set | Variable = Immediate Value Click here for the list of Variables Value that will be use along with the operand to change the address' value.

0xAF.name: SETVar
0xAF.description: Click here for the list of Variables Click here for the list of Variables

0xB0.description: Click here for the list of Variables Value that will be use along with the operand to change the address' value.

0xB1.name: AddVar
0xB1.description: Click here for the list of Variables Click here for the list of Variables

0xB2.name: Sub
0xB2.description: Click here for the list of Variables Value that will be use along with the operand to change the address' value.

0xB3.name: SubVar
0xB3.description: Click here for the list of Variables Click here for the list of Variables

0xB4.name: Mul
0xB4.description: Use this instruction for general multiplication. Click here for the list of Variables Value that will be use along with the operand to change the address' value.

0xB5.name: MulVar
0xB5.description: Use this instruction for general multiplication. Click here for the list of Variables Click here for the list of Variables

0xB6.name: Div
0xB6.description: Use this instruction for general division. Click here for the list of Variables Value that will be use along with the operand to change the address' value.

0xB7.description: Use this instruction for general division. Click here for the list of Variables Click here for the list of Variables

0xB8.name: Mod
0xB8.description: Divides the selected Variable Value by the Immediate Value and gives the So if the Variable Value was 0018 and the Immediate Value was 0006, the result would be 0000. If the Variable Value was 0019 and the Immediate Value was 0006, the result would be 0001.

0xB9.name: ModVar
0xB9.description: Divides Variable 1's Value by Variable 2's Value and gives the So if VAR1's Value was 0018 and VAR2's Value was 0006, the result (saved to VAR1) would be 0000. If VAR1's Value was 0019 and VAR2's Value was 0006, the result (saved to VAR1) would be 0001.

0xBA.name: And
0xBA.description: AND bitwise operand | Variable = Variable AND ImmediateValue This command compares the individual bits of the value within a Variable and a specific ImmediateValue, and produces a single binary output.  This makes this a good way to disable a specific bit within a byte without changing the rest of t...

0xBB.name: AndVar
0xBB.description: AND Variable bitwise operand | Variable1 = Variable1 AND Variable2 This command compares the individual bits of the value between two Variables, and produces a single binary output. With AND, the resulting bit outputs are set to 1 (TRUE) only if both values' bit inputs are 1. This makes this a good ...

0xBC.name: Or
0xBC.description: OR bitwise operand | Variable = Variable OR ImmediateValue This command compares the individual bits of the value between a Variable and a specific ImmediateValue, and produces a single binary output. With OR, the resulting bit outputs are set to 1 (TRUE) if either value's bit input is 1. This makes...

0xBD.name: OrVar
0xBD.description: OR Variable bitwise operand | Variable1 = Variable1 OR Variable2 This command compares the individual bits of the value between a Variable and a specific ImmediateValue, and produces a single binary output. With OR, the resulting bit outputs are set to 1 (TRUE) if either value's bit input is 1. This...

0xBF.name: UnknownBF
0xBF.description: This page is about the . For ASM random, see idk fill this in later

0xC1.name: LoadAddress
0xC1.description: Loads a value to a given variable. Works essentially the same as lbu/lhu/lw, but using variables. ID of the Variable which will be written to. Click here for the list of Variables

0xC2.name: UnitAddress
0xC2.description: Searches for the given Unit ID or Character Identity and returns the address to alter its stats. Returns 0 if no unit matched. to see what you can edit. ID of the unit specified in the ENTD.

0xC3.name: WorldUnitAddress
0xC3.description: Searches for the given Unit ID and returns the address to alter its world (non-battle) stats. Returns 0 if no unit matched. to see what you can edit. ID of the special unit or ID of the special dialogue assigned to that unit.

0xC4.name: SaveAddress
0xC4.description: Saves a variable to a given address. Works essentially the same as sb/sh/sw, but using variables. ID of the Variable which will be stored. Click here for the list of Variables

0xC5.name: ChangeDepthOfField
0xC5.description: Updates a unit's characteristics in special ways that cannot be easily achieved manually. Can currently only update statuses on a unit. Note: Can also affect whether a unit is controllable or not (Battle Stats variable x01BA). It's possible this works on many kinds of data in the Temporary Battle Of...

0xC6.name: TransformUnit
0xC6.description: Levels down a character and levels them back up to the same level as a new one, changing their Main ID, Job ID and Gender accordingly. Accurately preserves stats as much as possible (within the 3 byte raw stat length). There might be some loss of stats changing back and forth if one of the two jobs ...

0xC7.name: UnknownC7
0xC7.description: Variable 0x0000 = 1 if a unit is found, 0 otherwise If you do not specify the Main Roster or Bench as flags, then this instruction will always return 0! The Main ID or Name ID to search for.

0xC8.name: SetUnitTimer
0xC8.description: Set a timer on a specific unit (TIC exclusive)

0xC9.name: EnableChoiceMenu
0xC9.description: Enable the player choice menu overlay (TIC exclusive)

0xCA.name: SetNarrationMode
0xCA.description: Set narration mode for dialogue display (TIC exclusive)

0xCC.name: ResetTurnCounter
0xCC.description: Resets the turn counter of a specific unit or all units to use with the UnitTurns() Event Conditional.

0xCD.name: PostEvent
0xCD.description: Sets the action to take after an event has completed and no other event conditionals can successfully trigger. The Scenario List ID to load, as defined in ATTACK.OUT Editor Special Awesome. Leave as x0000 if not using "0x81: Load Next Scenario"

0xCE.name: LoadConditionals
0xCE.description: Temporarily loads a sector from CONDITIONALS.EVT (patched through the Event Conditionals v2+ spreadsheet) then overwrites the active conditionals of the event for up to 16 different conditional sets and 512 bytes of conditional data. This temporarily pauses the game for 0-1 frames while the sector i...

0xCF.name: Routine
0xCF.description: Runs/executes a routine at any address. r1 to r16 are automatically already freed and at your disposal. Routine Address : Word Address of the routine in raw hex to run/execute.

0xD0.name: SeekCodeForwardIfZero
0xD0.description: Jumps to {D2} ForwardTarget if Variable 0x0000 is equal to 0. Otherwise simply keeps advancing. Must be placed before {D2} ForwardTarget! Assigned ID from x00 to xFF so you have related instructions work only with this value to not create conflict.

0xD1.name: SeekCodeForward

params.name: jump_distance
params.description: Number of forward blocks to skip

0xD1.description: Jumps to {D2} ForwardTarget. Must be placed before {D2} ForwardTarget! Assigned ID from x00 to xFF so you have related instructions work only with this value to not create conflict.

0xD2.name: SeekCodeForwardTarget
0xD2.description: Target Destination of {D0} JumpForwardIfZero/{D1} JumpForward. Must be placed after {D0} JumpForwardIfZero/{D1} JumpForward! Assigned ID from x00 to xFF so you have related instructions work only with this value to not create conflict.

0xD3.name: SeekCodeBackward

params.name: jump_distance
params.description: Number of backward blocks to jump

0xD3.description: Jumps back to {D5} BackTarget. Must be placed after {D5} BackTarget! Assigned ID from x00 to xFF so you have related instructions work only with this value to not create conflict.

0xD4.name: Terminate
0xD4.description: Terminate the current event script execution

0xD5.name: SeekCodeBackwardTarget
0xD5.description: Target Destination of {D3} JumpBack. Must be placed before {D3} JumpBack! Assigned ID from x00 to xFF so you have related instructions work only with this value to not create conflict.

0xD6.name: ConditionalStart
0xD6.description: Begin a conditional expression block. Conditions follow until code block.

0xDB.name: _EventEnd
0xDB.description: End the current event and process scenario conditionals

0xDC.name: UnknownDC
0xDC.description: Unknown — stores 0x000D to a memory address

0xE0.name: GenerateUnit
0xE0.description: Generate a new unit using a simulated ENTD unit slot, overwriting an existing slot in the . Can generate a completely fresh unit, an "imposter" based on a unit in your roster, just a unit from your roster, or even just spit out a unit according to whatever preset data you've set up through . (The si...

0xE3.name: _EventEnd2
0xE3.description: A perfect copy of the other EventEnd. Marks the end of the event.

0xE4.name: UnknownE4
0xE4.description: Unknown — possibly related to event state

0xE5.name: _WaitForInstruction

params.name: instruction_id
params.description: ID of instruction to wait for

0xE5.description: Wait for any given instruction type instance to complete before resuming. This value is always set to x00 in the original game; it probably doesn't have a function.

0xE6.name: UnknownE6
0xE6.description: Dynamically alters a specified parameter of the next event instruction with a defined Variable's value. Does not affect AlterParameter instructions. If an AlterParameter instruction is encountered, the alteration will be passed on to the next instruction. This allows to stack multiple edits to the s...

0xE7.name: DisplayCaption
0xE7.description: Stores the Unit Address of the Unit present at the specified map coordinates into Variable 0. Returns 0 on fail. You can use this instruction to verify that a tile is unoccupied before performing mid-battle coordinate specific instructions. X Coordinate of the tile.

0xE8.name: LoadTileData
0xE8.description: Loads the tile data of a specified tile on the currently loaded map. ID of the Variable which will be written to. Click here for the list of Variables

0xE9.name: SaveTileData
0xE9.description: Saves the tile data of a specified tile on the currently loaded map. ID of the Variable which holds the value. Click here for the list of Variables

0xEA.name: LoadENTD
0xEA.description: Temporarily loads the raw ENTD data of a specified ENTD slot into from 0x801DF000 to 0x801DFFFF. Because of the volatile nature of this RAM section, it will be overwritten by an instruction, or any in-battle Effect. Optionally overwrites part of, or all units currently occupying the first 16 Battle ...

0xEB.name: MessageChain
0xEB.description: Loads an ENTD into into the current Event. Requires to be called beforehand in order to use. This instruction can be used to reroll units, or load completely new ones into the current event. If the unit is set to Party Level, you can even freely change what level they generate at mid-event by alteri...

0xEC.name: ConditionalDefeat
0xEC.description: Allows preventing automatic Game Overs on the Player team's defeat in order to create your own 'Player Defeated' events, or even make the Player's defeat part of the mod's storyline. Use the Defeat() Event Conditional to trigger the desired events. Without it, a battle will keep playing until anothe...

0xED.name: SetCharacterAlpha
0xED.description: Loads a database from the CD which was created by the Event Databases spreadsheet and subsequently patched with FFTOrgASM. , it allows to quickly load multiple variables with desired values with minimal event space use. Sector Offset of the desired Database as identified in the Event Databases sprea...

0xEE.name: UnknownEE
0xEE.description: Gets the size, or number of rows, of a table in the currently loaded database, so you can easily loop through all rows. Variable to store the number of rows the Table holds. Table ID as defined in the "Table Index" sheet of the Event Databases spreadsheet for the currently loaded Database.

0xEF.name: FetchTableRow
0xEF.description: Fetches a row of data of a previously loaded Database into the variables defined inside the database. , it allows to quickly load multiple variables with desired values with minimal event space use. Table ID as defined in the "Table Index" sheet of the Event Databases spreadsheet for the currently l...

0xF1.name: _Wait
0xF1.description: Final Fantasy Hacktics Wiki Event Instruction 0xf1: Wait This page was last edited on 25 April 2018, at 14:31.

0xF2.name: _Pad
0xF2.description: Doesn't do anything. Ideal for hex editing parts of the event out without any risk.
